toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run these tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host. 

To collect crash dumps when a test host crashes, I can use the `--crashdump` option (or `--blame-crash` depending on the test platform). Since this project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, it's using the newer Microsoft.Testing.Platform integration.

For the newer testing platform (Microsoft.Testing.Platform), the crash dump collection is done via `--crashdump` option.

For the older VSTest-based approach, it would be `--blame-crash`.

Since `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is `true`, this project uses the new testing platform. The flag for crash dump collection in this case is `--crashdump`.


Now I'll construct the command with the `--crashdump` flag to enable crash dump collection when running the tests.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-65409f5dff104176882cb72e05ce6f86 && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag isn't being recognized. This is because despite having `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` set to true, the `dotnet test` command is still going through MSBuild/VSTest. Let me check the dotnet version and try the correct approach.

For the newer Microsoft.Testing.Platform with `dotnet test`, I need to pass the option differently. The `--crashdump` is a testing platform option, and it might need to be passed with `--` separator or via a different mechanism.

Actually, looking at this more carefully:
- With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`, `dotnet test` should forward options to the testing platform


- But `--crashdump` needs to come after `--` to be passed to the test host

Let me try `dotnet test -- --crashdump`.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-65409f5dff104176882cb72e05ce6f86 && dotnet test -- --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.content:
All 3 tests passed with crash dump collection enabled. The command is:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

The `--` separator passes `--crashdump` through to the Microsoft Testing Platform (enabled via `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` in the `.csproj`). If a test crashes the host process, a dump file will be written to the test results directory for post-mortem debugging.
